text:
The House just voted to end the Iran war and rein in President Trump’s ability to keep taking military action against Iran without Congress signing off first. The resolution passed 215-208, with 4 Republicans joining every Democrat in voting yes. Those 4 Republicans are: 

Thomas Massie (Kentucky)
Brian Fitzpatrick (Pennsylvania)
Tom Barrett (Michigan)
Warren Davidson (Ohio)

It’s mostly symbolic and still needs the Senate to pass it — but it’s one of the clearest bipartisan rebukes of how Trump is handling the conflict yet.

Supporters say Congress should have more of a say in decisions about war. Opponents say the vote could hurt the administration’s position as it tries to negotiate a deal to end the fighting with Iran.
A war that NEVER should have began
